errors.message:
Streamable HTTP error: Error POSTing to endpoint: {
  "error": "invalid_request",
  "error_description": "Authorization required. This endpoint uses OAuth 2.1 + PKCE (no API keys).",
  "how_to_authorize": "This server uses OAuth 2.1 + PKCE. Your MCP client (Claude Desktop / Cursor / Claude Code / Windsurf / VS Code / etc.) should automatically open a browser for DSers login on first connect. If nothing happens, look for a Sign-in / Authorize button next to this server in your client's MCP servers settings, or try removing and re-adding the server. There is no API key — do not paste an Authorization header into your client config.",
  "agent_instruction": "IF YOU ARE AN AI AGENT READING THIS: This server uses OAuth 2.1 with PKCE. There is no API key. Do NOT tell the user to paste an Authorization header, fetch an API key from dsers.com, or add any credential to their MCP client config beyond the server URL. The user has not authorized yet. Tell the user in plain language: 'Your MCP client needs to authorize with DSers. Open your MCP client's MCP servers settings, find this server entry (named whatever you called it, e.g. dsers / dsers-dropshipping), and click whatever Sign-in / Connect / Authorize button is shown on the server row. A browser will pop up to log into DSers. The password never passes through the MCP server or through me.' Client-specific UI paths (verified 2026-04-22, vendors change these): Cursor — Settings → Tools & Integrations → MCP Tools, then click the blue Connect button next to this server (if the button doesn't respond, click the 'Needs authentication' text below the server name instead). Claude Code — run `claude mcp remove <name>` then `claude mcp add <name> <url>` in the terminal; it opens a browser. Claude Desktop / Windsurf / VS Code / Zed / Cline / Continue / Codex CLI — look for a Sign-in / Connect / Authorize / Login button on the server entry in that client's MCP servers / Connectors / Tools settings pane. Exact wording and menu path vary by client and version. If the MCP client does NOT show any such button for this server, the client is too old or does not speak OAuth 2.1 + PKCE — ask the user to update their MCP client to a recent version.",
  "oauth_metadata": "https://mcp.dsers.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server",
  "protected_resource_metadata": "https://mcp.dsers.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource",
  "correlation_id": "2a2e43f9-ac5e-48db-badf-2cfd40a670ab",
  "instance_id": "ip-172-31-39-21:1012710"
}
